solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int[] maximumCount(int[] nums, int[][] queries) {
        int n = nums.length;
        int q = queries.length;
        int[] res = new int[q];
        int[] primeCountPrefix = new int[n];
        int[] primeCountSuffix = new int[n];
        int[] primeCount = new int[n];
        int[] primeSet = new int[100001];
        boolean[] isPrime = new boolean[100001];
        isPrime[2] = true;
        for (int i = 3; i < 100001; i++) {
            isPrime[i] = true;
        }
        for (int i = 2; i * i < 100001; i++) {
            if (isPrime[i]) {
                for (int j = i * i; j < 100001; j += i) {
                    isPrime[j] = false;
                }
            }
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (isPrime[nums[i]]) {
                primeSet[nums[i]]++;
            }
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (isPrime[nums[i]]) {
                primeCount[i] = 1;
            }
            if (i > 0) {
                primeCount[i] += primeCount[i - 1];
            }
        }
        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            if (isPrime[nums[i]]) {
                primeCountSuffix[i] = 1;
            }
            if (i < n - 1) {
                primeCountSuffix[i] += primeCountSuffix[i + 1];
            }
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < q; i++) {
            int idx = queries[i][0];
            int val = queries[i][1];
            if (isPrime[nums[idx]]) {
                primeSet[nums[idx]]--;
            }
            if (isPrime[val]) {
                primeSet[val]++;
            }
            nums[idx] = val;
            int max = 0;
            for (int k = 1; k < n; k++) {
                int left = 0;
                int right = 0;
                for (int j = 0; j < k; j++) {
                    if (isPrime[nums[j]]) {
                        left++;
                    }
                }
                for (int j = k; j < n; j++) {
                    if (isPrime[nums[j]]) {
                        right++;
                    }
                }
                max = Math.max(max, left + right);
            }
            res[i] = max;
        }
        return res;
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n^2 + q*n)
space complexity: O(n + q)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution first precomputes prime numbers up to 100000 using the Sieve of Eratosthenes. For each query, it updates the array and recomputes the maximum sum of distinct prime counts for all possible splits. The main weakness is the O(n^2) time complexity due to recomputing prime counts for each split in each query.
